topic.title: Enabling maintenance mode
topic.para: Maintenance mode allows you to manually manage applications or services without interference from the cluster. You can put a resource, a node, or even the whole cluster into maintenance mode, depending on the scope of your planned changes.

When choosing the type of maintenance mode to use, keep in mind that your changes must be invisible to the cluster until you disable maintenance mode. Therefore, you must carefully evaluate any dependencies between the resources. For example, a resource could be in an ordering or colocation constraint with other resources, either on the same node or on different nodes.

listitem.para: If your changes might affect resources on multiple nodes, use cluster maintenance mode:
If your changes might affect multiple resources on one node, use node maintenance mode:
If your changes only affect one resource, use resource maintenance mode:

tip.para: You can put a group or a clone in resource maintenance mode if it has no dependencies with other resources outside the group or clone set.
